title: move to a solid, uv/uvx based deployment with a single entry point script and a legacy shim layer
description: until recently, we installed the python package with pip as root, which is not optimal, albeit it worked; we then thought of using the uv/uvx script feature to run standalone scripts with their ad-hoc venvs, but not all scripts in @bin are python scripts; for a transition period we use a bunch of aliases in @extra/siskin_aliases.sh to provide all the entry points; this all relates to the deployment, which is codified in @ansible; ultimately, we would like to have a single entry point script with a bunch of subcommands, e.g. siskin dir, siskin tree, siskin output, siskin status ... - but we have a mix of python and shell for some of the scripts, which blocked this approach; we want a solid single entry point script approach, but also want to keep the legacy commands around for compatibility

title: create a single entry point command siskin
description: we want a single entry point command named siskin, that would list all available subcommands etc; currently we have a mix of python / bash scripts to run the various tasks, as this was convenient and also fewer lines of code

title: make siskin gc safe and robust
description:
just stumbled upon /data/finc/siskin/89/IEEEIntermediateSchema/xref.doi
/data/finc/siskin/89/IEEEIntermediateSchema/release_extid.tsv.gz
/data/finc/siskin/89/IEEEIntermediateSchema/release_extid.tsv.zst
/data/finc/siskin/89/IEEEIntermediateSchema/fatcat.doi
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/finc/.local/bin/siskin", line 10, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
             ^^^^^^
  File "/home/finc/.local/share/uv/tools/siskin/lib64/python3.12/site-packages/siskin/cli.py", line 906, in main
    COMMANDS[subcmd]()
  File "/home/finc/.local/share/uv/tools/siskin/lib64/python3.12/site-packages/siskin/cli.py", line 617, in cmd_gc
    total_bytes = sum(os.path.getsize(f) for f in matching_files)
                  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/home/finc/.local/share/uv/tools/siskin/lib64/python3.12/site-packages/siskin/cli.py", line 617, in <genexpr>
    total_bytes = sum(os.path.getsize(f) for f in matching_files)
                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "<frozen genericpath>", line 62, in getsize
FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: /data/finc/siskin/89/IEEEIntermediateSchema/xref.doi
 in general, we want to revisit siskin gc; it should just recommend deletions and should make clear, that it is the operators duty to actually delete the files

title: CI fails when asking for pytest
description:
$ which python
/usr/local/bin/python
$ which pytest
Cleaning up project directory and file based variables 00:01
ERROR: Job failed: exit code 1; it seems pytest is not installed, albeit it is listed as a optional-dependency in pyproject.toml and the pytest lib appears in the output of the uv sync --all-groups --all-extras command, is it a path issue maybe?

title: siskin cli bash completion
description: currently we have some autocomplete for the taskxxx scripts in @etc/bash_completion.d/siskin_completion.sh - it would be great, if the unified 'siskin' command would get subcommand completion and then for the subcommands the task name completion, similar to the existing one; we only need a completion script; if can live alongside @etc/bash_completion.d/siskin_completion.sh

title: create cron tab entries for the individual sources
description: When deployed, we can run "siskin run TASKNAME" to run a task. for the ansible deployment, we already have some regular commands in @ansible/roles/siskin/tasks/main.yml

title: osf to intermediate schema test issue
uv tool install in ansible seems to have no effect
description: ansible role for siskin uses uv tool install -U siskin ...

title: allow shell scripts to be run from a single python entry point, or rewrite shell scripts to python
description: currently, we use bash scripts in @bin which is overall a bit awkward; we want a single entry point, and we either rewrite the shell scripts in python or find a way to savely execute a bit of shell from python; either would be ok, what amounts to a simpler, more readable approach

title: use token authentication for fetching OSF author/contributors
description: currently in @siskin/sources/osf.py we used a personal token from the config file when fetching data from the api, see: OSFDownload

title: split up AIExport task for different sources
description: currently in @siskin/workflows/ai.py the AIExport task is a combined tasks for

title: create a compatibility layer for the current task... commands
description: important: documentation and other past things mention the classic taskxxx commands and we would need to have a compat layer for that; this may be done with aliases or shim scripts; it is important that this will be robust, as the user should not get any other experience using the tool, even though the underlying structure has changed

title: using uvx is a bit too slow, let's use uv tool install -U
description: running uvx on each siskin invocation is fast, but still some overhead, let's use uv tool install -U on deploy to install the latest version

title: make updating the version a single command
description: currenty, I need to change __version__ in @siskin/__init__.py and the version in @pyproject.toml and then also update the @ansible/roles/siskin/defaults/main.yml siskin version entry; create a simple, robust makefile task or whatever other helper is suitable to make updating the version simpler; as we use @.gitlab-ci.yml to deploy on tag, e.g. v1.2.3 - we could also try to look the version another way (and e.g. reduce the places where it appears, etc.

title: ansible siskin role, increase max number of open files
description:
we want to increase the number of open files as we hit a limit; these are the current limit on the machine: $ prlimit | grep NOFILE
NOFILE     max number of open files                1024    524288 files

title: kalliope cron fix
description: the kallipe task in the cron job in @ansible/roles/siskin/tasks/main.yml is outdated; use the new siskin command "siskin run" and also use a new variable "solr" that will point the current solr collection and I can set this in @ansible/roles/siskin/defaults/main.yml (you can set it to a placeholder)

title: roles/siskin/templates/siskin_aliases.sh.j2 should reflect the new approach, also expressed in @extra/siskin_aliases.sh
description: while deploying the application, i noticed the aliases did not get updated; we should adjust the template used by ansible @roles/siskin/templates/siskin_aliases.sh.j2 to reflect @extra/siskin_aliases.sh; it is ok if these two exists for the moment
